title: Melastoma tococa
intro: Melastoma tococa, also known as Tococa, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Melastomataceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as fever, colds, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Melastoma tococa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal infusions, teas, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health issues, as well as being used in folk medicine for its supposed anti-diarrheal and antipyretic properties.
taxonomy: Melastoma tococa is classified within the family Melastomataceae. It belongs to the genus Melastoma. This plant is native to the tropical regions of Asia. Melastoma tococa is a flowering plant, specifically a shrub or small tree. As a member of the Melastomataceae family, it is part of the order Myrtales in the Angiosperm phylogeny.
